content: Federico Baldassarre is a postdoctoral researcher at FAIR in Meta AI, Paris. His current interests include self-supervised learning, computer vision, and world models. During his PhD at KTH, Stockholm, he worked on the explainability of deep learning models, applied to both computer vision and protein folding.
summary: Federico Baldassarre is a postdoctoral researcher at FAIR in Meta AI, Paris. His current interests include self-supervised learning, computer vision, and world models. During his PhD at KTH, Stockholm, he worked on the explainability of deep learning models, applied to both computer vision and protein folding.
title: Federico Baldassarre

summary: **ICASSP 2023** - Control the bit rate allocation of a a PQ-VAE using semantic information from DINO, in collaboration with Meta AI in Paris.
title: Variable Rate Allocation for Vector-Quantized Autoencoders

summary: **ECML PKDD 2022** - Learnable masked tokens reduce overfitting when training transformers in low-data regime.
title: Learnable Masked Tokens for Improved Transferability of Self-Supervised Vision Transformers

summary: **BMVC 2022** - Quantitative metrics for evaluating explanations of video DeepFake detectors, in collaboration with the Huawei Ireland Research Center.
title: Quantitative Metrics for Evaluating Explanations of Video DeepFake Detectors

summary: **ICLR Workshop 2022** - How to learn object-centric representations using contrastive learning on multi-object datasets?
title: Towards Self-Supervised Learning of Global and Object-Centric Representations

summary: **Proteins: Structure, Function, and Bioinformatics** - CASP community-wide experiment on modeling SARS-CoV-2 proteins causing the COVID-19 pandemic.
title: Modeling SARS-CoV-2 proteins in the CASP-commons experiment

summary: **ECCV 2020** - A novel weakly-supervised method for visual relationship detection that relies on minimal image-level predicate labels, graph networks, and attribution-based explanations.
title: Explanation-based Weakly-supervised Learning of Visual Relations with Graph Networks

summary: **Bioinformatics 2020** - A novel method for protein quality assessment based on a graphical representation of tertiary structures and graph convolutional networks.
title: GraphQA: Protein Model Quality Assessment Using Graph Convolutional Networks

summary: **ICML Workshop 2019** - How do Sensitivity Analysis, Guided Backpropagation, and Layer-Wise Relevance Propagation behave when applied to Graph Neural Networks?
title: Explainability Techniques for Graph Convolutional Networks

summary: **Master's Thesis** - Given the picture of a person and of a clothing item, generate the image of the person wearing it. Work performed during an internship at Zalando Research, Berlin.
title: Morphing Architectures for Pose-based Image Generation of People in Clothing

summary: **Technical Report** - Grayscale image colorization using a CNN conditione on Inception-ResNet-v2. Deep Learning course at KTH, best project award.
title: Deep Koalarization

content:
Sept 2023 I join the DINO team at Meta AI in Paris as a postdoctoral researcher 🦖
Jun 2023 PhD Graduation 🎓🎉
Feb 2023 Our work on semantic image compression with quantized autoencoders is accepted at ICASSP 2023 (Meta AI internship) .
Sept 2022 Our work on quantitative metrics for evaluating DeepFake explanations is accepted at BMVC 2022 (Huawei Research internship).
Aug 2022 My master student Erik Dao graduated with a thesis on sparsity in transformers for object detection (DETR).
Mar 2022 Our work on object-centric learning is accepted at the ICLR 2022 Workshop on the Elements of Reasoning: Objects, Structure, and Causality.
Feb 2022 Accepted a research internship at FAIR (Meta AI) in Paris under the supervision of Hervé Jégou.
Jan 2022 Gave a presentation on evaluating DeepFake explanations at Digital Future’s Machine Learning Day.
Sept 2020 Started a summer internship at Huawei Research in Dublin!
Jan 2021 My master student Jindong Wu graduated with a thesis on pooling strategies for Graph Networks.
Aug 2020 Received an award for being an outstanding reviewer at ECCV 2020.
Jun 2020 Hosted a full-day workshop for my department on how use our in-house GPU cluster.
May 2019 Our work on GNN Explainability is accepted at the ICML 2019 Workshop on Learning and Reasoning with Graph-Structured Data.
Jan 2019 Presented my Master’s thesis on image generation for clothes virtual try-on at the Northern Lights Deep Learning Workshop.
Sept 2018 Starting my PhD journey at KTH under the supervision of Prof. Hossein Azizpour.
summary:
Sept 2023 I join the DINO team at Meta AI in Paris as a postdoctoral researcher 🦖
Jun 2023 PhD Graduation 🎓🎉
Feb 2023 Our work on semantic image compression with quantized autoencoders is accepted at ICASSP 2023 (Meta AI internship) .
title: News
